paper_title: Genetic variation of Nang Thom Cho Dao rice variety based on whole genome sequencing

entities.description: The main research problem focused on the genomic differences of Nang Thom Cho Dao rice.
The application of genetic markers to improve rice crops.
The primary field of research involving whole genome sequencing.
The process of determining the complete DNA sequence of the rice genome.
The analytical workflow used to detect and favor genetic variants.
The method used for genomic DNA extraction from immature leaves.
The sequencing platform used for full genome re-sequencing.
An ultrafast FASTQ preprocessor for quality checking.
Software used for mapping quality-filtered reads to the reference genome.
Toolkit used for variant detection and eliminating low-mapping quality.
Tool used for functional annotation of genetic variants.
The reference rice genome database used for comparison.
Total volume of sequencing reads analyzed.
Total size of the sequencing data generated.
Percentage of NTCD sequence mapped to the Nipponbare genome.
The mean sequencing depth across the reference genome.
The ratio of transition to transversion mutations.
Total number of insertion and deletion variants detected.
